title: Judgment Genome Extraction v3.0
description: Complete structured extraction from a legal judgment using the Judgment Genome Project™ Framework v3.0 with embedded verification architecture.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the schema this document conforms to

extraction_date.description: ISO date when extraction was performed

source_url.description: URL of the judgment source (e.g., Indian Kanoon, SCC Online)

extractor.description: Identifier of the extracting agent (AI model or human)

dimension_1_visible.description: DIMENSION 1: THE VISIBLE — What the judgment says

case_name.description: Full case name: Party v. Party

strength.description: Number of judges on the bench

case_origin.description: How the case reached this court

source_paragraphs.description: ¶ numbers from which identity fields were extracted

verification_note.description: Confirmation that both versions are factually identical

date.description: ISO date or null if not stated in judgment

provisions_engaged.description: Section 1.4 — All legal provisions engaged in the judgment

provision_id.description: Section number and name, e.g., 'Section 464 IPC — Making a False Document'

exact_text.description: Quoted from judgment's reproduction, or null if not reproduced

corresponding_new_provision.description: Gate 5 — Cross-statutory mapping if parent statute repealed/replaced

precedent_registry.description: Section 1.5 — All cases cited in the judgment

what_it_held.description: 2-3 sentences — ONLY what current judgment says it held

linked_syllogism_ids.description: IDs of syllogisms from 2.1 that produce this ratio

necessity_check.description: Verification: Is this truly obiter or should it be ratio?

operative_order.description: Section 1.8 — What the court actually ordered

dimension_2_structural.description: DIMENSION 2: THE STRUCTURAL — How the reasoning is built

syllogisms.description: Section 2.1 — Logical DNA of the judgment

is_independent.description: True if this syllogism stands alone; false if it depends on another syllogism's conclusion

feeds_into.description: IDs of syllogisms this conclusion feeds into (can be multiple)

chain_logic.description: Narrative description of how syllogisms connect

dependency_tree.description: Section 2.2 — Flat dependency tree with parent references

node_id.description: Unique node ID within this tree

parent_node_id.description: ID of parent node. null = root-level dependency.

branch_id.description: Which branch this node belongs to (e.g., BRANCH_A)

is_critical.description: True = removal collapses the branch; False = merely supportive

method_basis.description: Did the judge explain the choice or was it implicit?

alternative_method_analysis.description: What if a different method were used? Must SHOW steps, not just assert.

analysis_steps.description: Step-by-step application of the alternative method. Minimum 2 steps.

ambiguity_type.description: Distinguish judge's ambiguity from extractor's uncertainty

dimension_3_invisible.description: DIMENSION 3: THE INVISIBLE — What the judgment hides. ALL items carry confidence markers.

is_truly_unstated.description: Confirmed court did not state this?

silent_arguments.description: Section 3.2 — Arguments never made but should have been. Each MUST include stress test.

provision_or_principle.description: EXACT section number + statute name. Gate 4 Step 1.

dependent_syllogism_ids.description: IDs of ALL syllogisms from 2.1 that depend on this fact

is_practically_plausible.description: Reality check: would a practitioner encounter this variation?

intent_vs_definition_confusion.description: True if this interpretation confuses intent requirements with definitional expansion

supporting_sc_judgment.description: At least one SC judgment endorsing the competing doctrine

dimension_4_weaponizable.description: DIMENSION 4: THE WEAPONIZABLE — How to use/attack this judgment

key_factual_similarity.description: What MUST be true for this shield to work

boundary_condition.description: Under what facts would this shield FAIL?

stress_test.description: Gate 4 stress test applied to the vulnerability

is_material_to_ratio.description: Validity check: does the ratio depend on this fact?

cross_domain_transplants.description: Section 4.5 — Only items that PASSED Gate 7

target_provision.description: Specific section + statute in target domain

cross_statutory_mapping.description: Gate 5 — Required if the statute has been repealed/replaced

dimension_5_synthesis.description: DIMENSION 5: THE SYNTHESIS — Pulling everything together

interpretive_lineage_map.description: Section 5.1 — One entry per provision interpreted

source.description: Whether this case was cited in the judgment or added by the extractor

genome_summary.description: Section 5.2 — ONE paragraph capturing the complete genome + consistency verification

text.description: 5-8 sentences capturing holding, assumptions, vulnerability, trajectory, value

questions_created.description: Section 5.4 — NEW questions this judgment creates

why_new.description: Why it didn't exist before this judgment

constitutional_reference.description: Gate 6 — Required if the question involves constitutional rights

dimension_6_audit.description: DIMENSION 6: THE AUDIT — Mandatory self-verification report

document_id.description: Globally unique extraction ID (UUID or citation-based hash)

_search_hints.description: Non-validated hints for downstream search/indexing systems
